headline: Ghana Plants 31 Million Trees, Restores Vast Lands
meta_description: Ghana planted over 31 million trees in 2025, restoring more than 23,600 hectares of degraded land as part of its Tree for Life program.
summary_short: Ghana's ambitious tree-planting drive has restored vast areas of degraded land, with 31 million trees planted in a single year.
body:
Ghana completed an impressive environmental restoration project in 2025, planting more than 31 million trees and restoring over 23,600 hectares of degraded land. This major achievement is part of Ghana's national Tree for Life program, which has demonstrated the scale of impact possible when countries commit sustained resources to forest restoration.

The scale of the effort shows what organized, nationwide action can accomplish. Restoring degraded lands through reforestation improves soil health, increases biodiversity, provides livelihood opportunities for workers, and contributes to carbon sequestration.

Looking forward, Ghana has set an even more ambitious target, planning to plant another 30 million trees in the coming year. This sustained commitment reflects a long-term vision for the country's ecological recovery and demonstrates that large-scale environmental restoration is achievable when supported by government leadership and community participation.
